test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

claude-opus-4-8.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language for the JVM that interoperates fully with Java and is now the preferred language for Android development.
2. Elixir (2011) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang virtual machine designed for scalable and maintainable applications.
3. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language developed by Google that powers the Flutter framework for building cross-platform applications.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that adds optional static typing to the language.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language aimed at numerical and scientific computing.
6. Swift (2014) - A language created by Apple for building applications across its iOS, macOS, and other platforms.
7. Zig (2016) - A low-level systems language focused on simplicity, performance, and explicit control over memory.

claude-sonnet-4-6.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed, JVM-compatible language developed by JetBrains that blends object-oriented and functional programming paradigms.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language created by Google for building fast, cross-platform applications, most notably used with the Flutter framework.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A statically typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles to plain JavaScript and adds optional type annotations.
4. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang VM, designed for building scalable, fault-tolerant distributed systems.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language designed primarily for numerical and scientific computing, offering speeds comparable to statically typed languages.
6. Swift (2014) - An open-source compiled language created by Apple to replace Objective-C for developing applications across Apple platforms.
7. Zig (2016) - A general-purpose systems programming language designed as a modern alternative to C, emphasizing explicit memory control and no hidden control flow.

gpt-5-5.modelOutput:
1. Dart (2011) - A client optimized language created by Google for building web, server, and mobile applications.
2. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language from JetBrains designed to interoperate with Java and improve developer productivity.
3. Elixir (2012) - A functional language built on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable and fault tolerant systems.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to JavaScript for large scale application development.
5. Julia (2012) - A high performance language for numerical computing, data science, and scientific programming.
6. Swift (2014) - A modern language from Apple for building software across its platforms with safety and performance in mind.
7. Crystal (2014) - A compiled language with Ruby inspired syntax designed for static typing and efficient execution.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed programming language developed by JetBrains that targets the Java Virtual Machine and compiles to Java bytecode.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized programming language developed by Google for building fast apps on multiple platforms.
3. Elixir (2012) - A dynamic functional programming language built on the Erlang VM for creating scalable and maintainable applications.
4. Elm (2012) - A functional programming language that compiles to JavaScript and emphasizes simplicity with guarantees of no runtime exceptions.
5. TypeScript (2012) - A statically typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles down to plain JavaScript.
6. Swift (2014) - A compiled programming language created by Apple for developing applications across iOS, macOS, and other platforms.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed compiled programming language with Ruby-like syntax focused on high performance and developer productivity.
